summary: GPS Check-in Fix - All 8 backend tests passed. Verified: (1) No alert() calls in InvitationPage.js or AppointmentDetail.js, (2) toast imported from sonner in both files, (3) Backend returns proper HTTP codes (404 for invalid token, 400 for non-accepted participant, 409 for duplicate check-in), (4) Frontend uses toast.warning for GPS errors (PERMISSION_DENIED, POSITION_UNAVAILABLE, TIMEOUT), toast.info for 409, toast.error for 400/404, toast.success for success.

backend: Test 2: POST /api/checkin/manual with invalid token returns 404 with detail 'Invitation invalide'
Test 3: POST /api/checkin/manual with non-accepted participant returns 400 with detail 'Vous devez avoir accepté l'invitation pour effectuer un check-in'
Test 4: POST /api/checkin/manual accepts valid check-in without GPS coordinates
Test 5: POST /api/checkin/manual accepts valid check-in with GPS coordinates (lat/lon)
Test 6: POST /api/checkin/manual returns 409 for duplicate check-in
Test 7: POST /api/checkin/gps with invalid token returns 404
Test 8: POST /api/checkin/gps returns 409 for duplicate GPS evidence
frontend: No alert() calls found in InvitationPage.js
No alert() calls found in AppointmentDetail.js
toast imported from sonner in InvitationPage.js (line 5)
toast imported from sonner in AppointmentDetail.js (line 6)

backend_logging.manual_checkin: logger.info/warning with participant_id, apt_id, gps_consent, lat, lon
backend_logging.gps_checkin: logger.info/warning with participant_id, apt_id, lat, lon, distance_km

seed_data_creation: Created temporary test appointments and participants for each test - cleaned up after each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: GPS check-in fix fully verified. Key implementation: (1) InvitationPage.js handleManualCheckin (lines 282-342) handles GeolocationPositionError codes 1/2/3 with specific French toast.warning messages, maps HTTP 409/400/404 to toast.info/error. (2) AppointmentDetail.js handleOrganizerCheckin (lines 128-187) has identical GPS error handling. (3) Backend checkin_routes.py has logging via logger.info/warning. (4) No alert() calls remain in either file - all replaced with sonner toast messages.
